name: Peter's denial, foretold and fulfilled
notes: All four Gospels record both halves: Jesus foretelling that Peter will deny him thrice before the cock crows, and Peter doing it in the high priest's courtyard. The weave links the four predictions to one another, the four fulfilments to one another, and — within each Gospel — the prediction to the moment Peter "remembered the word" (Matt 26:75; Mark 14:72; Luke 22:61; John 18:27), so the foretelling and its fulfilment line up. The accusers differ in detail (a damsel, another maid, the bystanders who hear his Galilean speech; John names the doorkeeper and a kinsman of Malchus), but all four hold the count at three denials. Luke alone records the Lord turning to look at Peter (22:61).
